summary: This cluster synthesizes research on the cultivation and seedling production of commercially valuable red seaweeds, particularly eucheumatoid species, through advanced micropropagation techniques. The papers collectively focus on the use of tissue culture, callus culture, and protoplast culture to enhance seedling quality and scalability, with biostimulants employed to improve growth and resilience. Key findings emphasize the critical role of acclimatization protocols in mitigating disease and herbivory during the transition from land-based nurseries to open-sea grow-out systems. The geographic scope is global, reflecting efforts to optimize sustainable farming practices for tropical and temperate seaweed aquaculture.
entity_sample: changing the seasonal rhythms of seaweed cultivation
